subagent_reports.topic: Cross-project change workflow start — opportunity scouting within approved scope

sources.label: change.ts cross-project create path
sources.summary: Lone outlier: cross-project create uses createLegacyStore(target_path) (disk store, no workflow start). Comment at L787-789 confirms 'Temporal runtime is not initialized for external repos in this tool path.' Calls targetStore.changes.create + save with NO ensureChangeWorkflowStarted/startChangeWorkflow.

sources.label: Temporal-backed store create already starts workflow
sources.summary: Same-project (Temporal) create path already does getTemporalWorkflowClient()+ensureChangeWorkflowStarted with full seedState AND a P1.4 transactional rollback guard (removeChangeDir on start failure, rethrow original error). This is the exact pattern the proposal asks to add.

sources.label: withTargetPathStore Temporal mode
sources.summary: Default stateRequirement (not snapshot-ok/scaffold) builds a Temporal-backed target store via createStore({temporalBundle}) + init(), after ensureProjectTemporalQueue(projectId). task/gate/contract/subagent-report/temporal-ops cross-project tools ALL route through this; change-create is the only cross-project mutation NOT using it.

sources.label: reseedChangeFromDisk reconciliation primitive
sources.summary: P1.5 orphan-tolerant getTemporalChange already auto-seeds disk-only non-terminal changes into workflows ON READ via reseedChangeFromDisk (ensureChangeWorkflowStarted with changeSeedStateFromChange). Archived/closed short-circuit (no re-seed). The reconciliation engine the proposal scopes already exists.

sources.label: startChangeWorkflow adapter
sources.summary: Idempotent thin wrapper over ensureChangeWorkflowStarted; documented 'returns existing handle if already running'. Available for direct reuse; satisfies proposal idempotency constraint.

sources.label: rq-statusCliWorkerFree01 spec
sources.summary: Confirms status active rows come from Temporal Visibility search attributes upserted by change workflows, fail-closed, no disk active fallback. A disk-only change has no Visibility attrs → invisible. Root cause confirmed by spec.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Root cause and fix are convergence, not new machinery. Every primitive the proposal scopes already exists and is exercised by sibling tools: (1) Temporal-backed target store (withTargetPathStore default mode) that auto-starts the workflow on create via changes.ts:182 with a built-in P1.4 rollback guard; (2) idempotent startChangeWorkflow/ensureChangeWorkflowStarted adapters; (3) reseedChangeFromDisk + P1.5 orphan-tolerant read that already reconciles disk-only active changes into workflows on the first Temporal-store read. The cross-project create path is the single outlier still using createLegacyStore directly. The lowest-risk by-the-book fix is to route cross-project create through withTargetPathStore (default Temporal stateRequirement) exactly as task/gate/contract already do, inheriting workflow-start + rollback + cache-refresh + trust-gating for free, instead of hand-rolling a second ensureChangeWorkflowStarted call site in change.ts. This also removes the existing trust-gating asymmetry (change-create currently does its own validateCrossRepoTarget rather than the shared resolveTargetProject mutation gate). Proposal constraints are satisfied: workflow.start is not a synchronous target query; idempotency is guaranteed by ensureChangeWorkflowStarted; Visibility-backed worker-free status semantics are preserved (workflow start upserts search attributes server-side). Reconciliation for pre-existing orphans (reworkRelatedCards) is LARGELY ALREADY SOLVED by reseedChangeFromDisk on read — discovery should verify whether a dedicated startup scan is even needed or whether first Temporal-store read already heals it, avoiding a redundant new scan surface.

validation.notes: No source-access blockers, no contract/security/release blockers, no contradictory evidence. All findings code/spec-cited in-repo.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt the convergence framing in design: (a) prefer routing cross-project change-create through withTargetPathStore default Temporal mode to reuse the existing workflow-start + P1.4 rollback + trust gate, rather than adding a parallel ensureChangeWorkflowStarted call in change.ts (CANDIDATE-1, user-surface: architectural direction); (b) before designing a new bounded reconciliation scan, verify whether existing reseedChangeFromDisk/P1.5 read-time healing already makes disk-only orphans visible once their target project opens a Temporal-store session — this may shrink reconciliation scope to docs+test-only (CANDIDATE-2, user-surface); (c) auto-adopt reuse of the idempotent startChangeWorkflow adapter and changeSeedStateFromChange seed builder rather than constructing seedState inline (CANDIDATE-3, auto-adopt).

sources.label: Cross-project create outlier (disk store, no workflow start)
sources.summary: createLegacyStore(target_path)+changes.create+save with NO ensureChangeWorkflowStarted. Comment L787-789 confirms 'Temporal runtime is not initialized for external repos in this tool path.' Source-side cross_project_origin enrichment (L815-817) and cross_project_links write (L828-849) live here and must be preserved if routing changes.

sources.label: Same-project Temporal create already starts workflow + P1.4 rollback
sources.summary: getTemporalWorkflowClient()+ensureChangeWorkflowStarted with full seedState. P1.4 guard removeChangeDir on start failure and re-throws ORIGINAL error. This ROLLS BACK on failure — it does NOT keep disk+warn.

sources.label: withTargetPathStore default Temporal mode
sources.summary: Default stateRequirement (not snapshot-ok/scaffold) runs ensureProjectTemporalQueue + createStore({temporalBundle}) + init(), returns stateMode:'temporal'. resolveTargetProject mutation gate applied (L168-174). task/gate/contract/report cross-project tools route through this; change-create does not.

sources.label: reseedChangeFromDisk reconciliation primitive
sources.summary: Non-terminal disk-only change → ensureChangeWorkflowStarted(changeSeedStateFromChange(change)). Archived/closed short-circuit to disk projection without re-creating workflow (AC5 'do not backfill terminal' already enforced).

sources.label: List-time + read-time self-healing drives reconciliation for ALL active orphans
sources.summary: getTemporalChange triggers reseedChangeFromDisk on missing-workflow fallback. listResolvedChanges unions disk IDs and loads each via getTemporalChange (L700) — so adv_status/adv_change_list against the target Temporal store already reseeds every non-terminal disk-only orphan. No new startup-scan surface required for AC5/AC6.

sources.label: Trust-gating asymmetry
sources.summary: Cross-project create uses validateCrossRepoTarget; withTargetPathStore uses resolveTargetProject mutation gate. If create is rerouted, the two trust checks must be proven equivalent before collapsing (C3 constraint).

sources.label: Status worker-free Visibility law
sources.summary: Status active rows come from Temporal Visibility search attributes upserted by change workflows; fail-closed, no disk active fallback. Disk-only change = no Visibility attrs = invisible. workflow.start upserts attributes server-side without a live worker, satisfying AC2/SC4.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Every primitive the design needs already exists and is exercised by sibling cross-project tools; the fix is convergence onto the existing target-path mutation architecture, not new machinery (satisfies DONT4). The single outlier is cross-project create still using createLegacyStore directly. Routing it through withTargetPathStore default Temporal mode inherits workflow-start + P1.4 rollback + resolveTargetProject trust gate + cache refresh, exactly as task/gate/contract do. For reconciliation: list-time + read-time reseed already self-heals ALL non-terminal disk-only orphans on the next adv_status/adv_change_list through the target Temporal store, so AC5/AC6 likely need NO new scan surface — design should make this the deliberate, tested trigger rather than building a redundant startup scan (DONT3). One genuine contradiction the design MUST resolve: proposal text says 'keep disk projection + warn' on Temporal-start failure, but the existing same-project P1.4 guard ROLLS BACK (removeChangeDir). Agreement AC4 + decision #1 already chose rollback ('no active disk-only orphan remains'), so P1.4 parity is the correct path and the proposal's keep+warn wording is stale — confirm design follows AC4, not proposal prose.

validation.notes: No source-access, contract, security, or release blockers. All candidates code/spec-cited in-repo. AC4 vs proposal-prose contradiction is resolved by the approved agreement (rollback); flagged for design to state explicitly.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt convergence framing: (C1, user-surface direction) reroute cross-project create through withTargetPathStore default Temporal mode as primary architecture vs hand-rolled ensureChangeWorkflowStarted in change.ts, preserving source-side cross_project_origin + cross_project_links writes; (C2, auto-adopt) treat existing list/read-time reseedChangeFromDisk as the reconciliation trigger and add bounded latency/safety tests rather than a new startup scan; (C3, auto-adopt) reuse startChangeWorkflow adapter + changeSeedStateFromChange seed builder; (C4, auto-adopt) follow P1.4 rollback semantics per AC4 and drop stale proposal keep+warn wording; (C5, user-surface) if C1 adopted, verify validateCrossRepoTarget == resolveTargetProject trust equivalence before collapsing.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for startCrossProjectChange (cross-project change workflow registration)

sources.label: createCrossProjectFollowUp source
sources.summary: Confirms current divergence: uses createLegacyStore (L790), calls changes.create then changes.get + patches cross_project_origin via changes.save (L813-817). Never starts a target change workflow. This is the exact gap the design targets.

sources.label: Temporal store changes.create (rollback semantics)
sources.summary: Temporal-backed create scaffolds disk via legacy then ensureChangeWorkflowStarted; on failure removeChangeDir + rethrow ORIGINAL error (P1.4 transactional guard). Validates KD1/KD4/AC4 reuse claim. seedState (L190-204) currently omits cross_project_origin, confirming KD2 work item.

sources.label: reseedChangeFromDisk (reconciliation trigger)
sources.summary: Non-terminal disk projections reseed via ensureChangeWorkflowStarted + changeSeedStateFromChange (L437-445); archived/closed short-circuit to disk projection WITHOUT workflow start (L422-424). getTemporalChange classifies fallback and calls reseed. Validates KD5/AC5 with no new scanner.

sources.label: withTargetPathStore / TargetStateRequirement
sources.summary: withTargetPathStore exists with trust gating, confirmation, shard routing. TargetStateRequirement enum is 'snapshot-ok' | 'temporal-required' | 'scaffold' — there is NO 'temporal' value. Design's 'stateRequirement: temporal' is an imprecise label; correct value is 'temporal-required'.

sources.label: ChangeCreateInitialMetadata + changeSeedStateFromChange
sources.summary: ChangeCreateInitialMetadata currently carries origin | fast_follow_of | scope_repos (no cross_project_origin). changeSeedStateFromChange exists. Confirms KD2 must extend metadata + seed picklist; replay-safe optional-field approach matches existing pattern.

sources.label: Spec: CLI Status Reads Visibility Worker-Free
sources.summary: Active rows MUST come from Temporal Visibility search attributes and fail closed (no disk-projected active rows). Design KD7 explicitly preserves this; no contradiction.

sources.label: Spec: cross-project target_path routing + shard routing
sources.summary: Existing law: target_path validation, untrusted-target confirmation, cross_project_links persistence, target store ownership of signals/cache (task mutations), canonical target shard routing (fixTargetPathSharding). Design KD1-KD3 align with and extend this contract; no conflict.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Design converges a genuinely divergent path (createCrossProjectFollowUp using createLegacyStore + post-create disk patch) onto the proven Temporal-backed target-store create machinery that already provides ensureChangeWorkflowStarted, Visibility search-attribute registration, and P1.4 rollback-on-start-failure. Every structural anchor cited in the design (withTargetPathStore, Temporal changes.create rollback, reseedChangeFromDisk archived/closed short-circuit, ChangeCreateInitialMetadata, changeSeedStateFromChange, CHANGE_LIST_BATCH_SIZE=20) is verified present in source. The reconciliation choice (KD5: reuse existing list/read reseed rather than a new scanner) is the materially simpler option and is already implemented and tested-adjacent. No spec-law contradiction found; design preserves worker-free Visibility status (advance-meta) and target shard routing (advance-workflow).

validation.notes: One non-blocking naming imprecision: design references withTargetPathStore stateRequirement 'temporal', but the actual TargetStateRequirement enum value is 'temporal-required' (target-project.ts:16-19). Cosmetic; implementer should use the real enum value. Second non-blocking gap: KD2 says extend ChangeWorkflowState seed + changeSeedStateFromChange to carry cross_project_origin replay-safe, but the design does not state how the Temporal store create path (changes.ts L190-204, which builds seedState inline, NOT via changeSeedStateFromChange) will pass cross_project_origin through — that inline seed block also needs cross_project_origin wired, or the create path must route through changeSeedStateFromChange. Worth an explicit prep task.

subagent_reports.recommendation: VALIDATED with two minor cautions. The design is correct, evidence-backed, and notably simple: it removes a bespoke divergent path and reuses existing target-store + Temporal-create + reseed machinery, satisfying AC1-AC8 and all constraints without new scanners or duplicated workflow-start code. Before planning, resolve two precision items: (1) replace stateRequirement label 'temporal' with the real enum 'temporal-required'; (2) make explicit that the Temporal store changes.create inline seedState block (changes.ts ~L190-204) must also carry cross_project_origin, not only changeSeedStateFromChange — otherwise KD2 leaves a seed gap on the primary create path. Neither item changes the architecture; both are implementation-precision tasks for prep.

## Cross-Project Origin

This change was created as a follow-up from **toolbox**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Source project | toolbox |
| Source path | `/home/jon/toolbox` |

> **Note:** The originating project should be consulted for context on why this change is needed.

# Proposal: Start cross-project change workflows

## Problem

Cross-project `adv_change_create` currently creates target-project changes as disk projections only. Those changes do not get Temporal workflows or Visibility search attributes, so live status and launcher surfaces miss them even though creation succeeded.

## What Changes

- Cross-project change creation registers the target project's change workflow after the disk projection is created.
- Existing disk-only target changes can be reconciled into Temporal-backed live status without changing their user-facing content.
- Temporal-unavailable cases fail clearly and roll back partial target scaffolding, matching same-project create semantics.

## User Outcomes

- Users who create follow-up changes in another ADV project see those changes in that target project's live status and launcher rows.
- Existing active disk-only changes become discoverable again from normal ADV status surfaces.
- Cross-project creation remains usable when the target project's worker is not running.
- Failures distinguish "not created" from "created and live-registered"; no silent disk-only active records are created on Temporal failure.

## Affected Code

- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` — cross-project `target_path` creation path.
- `plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts` — likely route cross-project create through the existing Temporal-backed target store helper.
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts` — existing read-time orphan reseed path may satisfy reconciliation if triggered deliberately.
- `plugin/src/temporal/workflow-start.ts` and adjacent Temporal-start helpers — idempotent workflow start/hydration behavior.
- Tests around cross-project create, Temporal workflow start, Visibility-backed status, and reconciliation.

## Related Repositories

- Current repo: `advance` — implementation and specs.
- Origin repo: `toolbox` — reported follow-up source; `/home/jon/toolbox` exists and is a project directory.
- Example impacted target: `pokeedge` — contains known disk-only `reworkRelatedCards` orphan used as validation context.

## Constraints

- Do not query target-project workflows synchronously from the source process.
- Preserve cross-project trust gating for `target_path` mutations.
- Preserve worker-free live status semantics: active rows come from Temporal Visibility, not disk fallback.
- Keep workflow start idempotent; repeated create/reconcile attempts must not duplicate workflows or fail because one already exists.
- Do not backfill archived or closed changes.

## Impact

- Improves reliability of cross-project follow-up creation and launcher visibility.
- Adds future-proof self-healing for active disk-only changes, not a one-time repair.
- May add a bounded reconciliation path; discovery found existing read-time reseed logic that can be reused instead of inventing a broad startup scanner.

## Context

Known evidence from existing code and specs:

- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` uses `createLegacyStore(target_path, ...)` in the cross-project path, then writes the target change projection.
- `plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts` already provides `withTargetPathStore(... stateRequirement: "temporal")`, which creates a Temporal-backed target store after target trust validation.
- Temporal-backed `changes.create` already calls `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` and has rollback behavior when workflow start fails.
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts` has `reseedChangeFromDisk` and `getTemporalChange` fallback that can start a missing workflow from disk for non-terminal changes.
- `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` starts workflows with `AdvChangeId`, `AdvChangeStatus`, and `AdvCurrentGate` search attributes and handles already-started workflows idempotently.
- `advance-meta` requirement `rq-statusCliWorkerFree01` requires CLI status active rows to come from Temporal Visibility and fail closed without disk active fallback.
- Archived `readCliStatusVisibility` implemented the worker-free Visibility row path for CLI status; current source has `loadLiveSummaries`/`summariesFromVisibility` for search-attribute summaries.
- Temporal TypeScript SDK docs show `workflow.start` takes `workflowId` and `taskQueue`; docs/source note one active workflow per ID and `WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStartedError`/conflict behavior for duplicate running IDs.

## Discovery Agenda

Resolved by discovery:

- Shared Temporal client is available through existing target-store routing (`withTargetPathStore` -> `getService()` -> `createStore`).
- Target project id and projection paths are already resolved in target-store context.
- Existing `reseedChangeFromDisk` can reconcile missing non-terminal workflows from disk on read; design must investigate exact trigger shape.
- User chose rollback/fail semantics when Temporal start fails after target scaffold begins.
- User chose all active disk-only records for reconciliation; archived/closed records remain untouched.

## Scope

### In Scope

- Start target Temporal workflow after cross-project target change projection is written, preferably by routing cross-project create through the existing Temporal-backed target store.
- Reconcile all active disk-only changes in a bounded, future-proof way, reusing existing reseed logic where safe.
- Add tests for workflow start, no target workflow query, idempotency, Temporal-down rollback/failure, and reconciliation.
- Update relevant specs/docs to state cross-project create live-registers target changes.

### Out of Scope

- Multi-namespace or multi-Temporal-server support.
- Changing worker-free Visibility-backed status semantics.
- Backfilling archived or closed changes.
- Changing launcher behavior beyond making target changes visible through existing status data.
- One-off manual repair limited only to `reworkRelatedCards`.

### Must Not

- Must not synchronously query a target workflow from the source project process.
- Must not treat disk fallback as live active status.
- Must not leave active disk-only target records on Temporal-start failure.
- Must not weaken `target_path` trust validation.

## Discovery Findings

### Discovery Checklist

| Step | Result | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Skills Considered | PASS | Loaded `adv-agent-tool-contracts`; no frontmatter pending-review skills found. |
| Prior Research Extension | PASS | No `temp/*.md`; cited `docs/repo-improve-prep.md` for Visibility/read-path context. |
| Conflict Scan | PASS | Active changes show no direct overlap; archived `readCliStatusVisibility` and `fixTargetPathSharding` are relevant prior work. `adv_change_validate` passed with expected pre-prep `NO_TASKS`/`NO_DELTAS` warnings. |
| Edge Case Investigation | PASS | Covered no target worker, Temporal unavailable, duplicate/already-started workflow, archived/closed disk records, untrusted target path, target shard routing. |
| Design Question Depth | PASS | User resolved behavior choices; technical trigger shape moves to design investigation. |
| Draft Spec Deltas | PASS | Add/adjust cross-project create law in `advance-workflow` or `advance-meta` with workflow-registration and reconciliation scenarios. |
| Related Pattern Scan | PASS | Cross-project create is the outlier; target mutation helpers already use Temporal-backed target stores. |
| LBP Check | PASS | Best direction is convergence on existing target-store/workflow-start machinery, not new bespoke Temporal client code. |

### Skills Considered

- `adv-agent-tool-contracts` — relevant; this changes an agent-callable tool behavior contract and must keep schema/preflight/tests/specs aligned.
- No new skill needed; core domain is existing ADV Temporal tool routing.

### Extends

- `docs/repo-improve-prep.md` — prior research validates Visibility-first read paths and warns about status/list hydration cost. New finding: cross-project creation has a write-side registration gap; fixing create reduces the need for status-side disk fallback and aligns with worker-free Visibility status.
- Archived `readCliStatusVisibility` — prior work made CLI status read active rows from search attributes; this change supplies missing workflows/search attributes for cross-project-created target changes.
- Archived `fixTargetPathSharding` — prior work fixed canonical target shard resolution; this change should reuse that target-path infrastructure and avoid caller-shard shadow writes.

### Conflict Scan

- Active related changes: none blocking. `updateTriageRelevanceChecks` is unrelated triage workflow work.
- Archived relevant work: `readCliStatusVisibility`, `fixTargetPathSharding`, `rewriteStatusCli`.
- Validation: passed; warnings are expected before planning (`NO_TASKS`, `NO_DELTAS`).
- Agenda overlap: several cross-project target cleanup/routing follow-ups exist, but none block this change.

### Current State

- `createCrossProjectFollowUp()` in `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` initializes a target `createLegacyStore`, writes the target change, patches `cross_project_origin`, and returns. It does not start a target workflow.
- `withTargetPathStore()` in `plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts` can create a Temporal-backed target store with target project id, canonical external root, target trust gating, and `ensureProjectTemporalQueue`.
- Temporal-backed `changes.create()` in `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts` starts the workflow via `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` and rolls back the disk scaffold if workflow start fails.
- `reseedChangeFromDisk()` in `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts` starts a missing workflow from a non-terminal disk projection and skips archived/closed records.
- CLI status has a worker-free `loadLiveSummaries()` path that builds rows from Visibility search attributes; no workflow means no row.

### Edge Cases

1. Target worker absent:
   - Create must not query target workflow state.
   - Workflow start can enqueue without waiting for worker execution; Visibility attrs must be present from start options or upsert soon enough for status.
2. Temporal unavailable:
   - User chose rollback/fail, matching same-project create.
   - Return must not claim create or live registration succeeded.
3. Already-started workflow:
   - Reconciliation and create retries must reuse/get existing handle or no-op, not throw user-visible duplicate errors.
   - Closed/archived workflow IDs must not be accidentally resurrected.
4. Disk-only old changes:
   - Active/non-terminal projections should be reseeded.
   - Archived/closed projections should remain terminal disk records; no workflow recreation.
5. Target path trust/sharding:
   - Untrusted mutation still requires explicit confirmation.
   - Target state must use target canonical shard, not caller shard.

### Open Design Questions

1. Reconciliation trigger
   - Trust model: agent-resolved by design investigation.
   - Blast radius: startup/status latency and whether old orphans self-heal without explicit read.
   - Alternatives: reuse read-time reseed only; add bounded active-disk scan on target status/session start; add explicit repair tool path.
2. Implementation route
   - Trust model: agent-only technical decision.
   - Blast radius: bespoke Temporal start code could duplicate target-store semantics.
   - Alternatives: route create through `withTargetPathStore(... temporal)`; direct `startChangeWorkflow`; direct `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` with manual target paths. Recommended: target-store route.

### Draft Spec Deltas

- `rq-crossProjectCreateWorkflow01` — Cross-project `adv_change_create target_path` registers the target Temporal change workflow and Visibility search attributes for non-terminal target changes.
  - Given a trusted target path and reachable Temporal service; when `adv_change_create` creates a target change; then a target workflow exists with `AdvChangeId`, `AdvChangeStatus`, `AdvCurrentGate`, and target `AdvAffectedProjects` search attributes.
- `rq-crossProjectCreateNoTargetQuery01` — Cross-project create must not issue synchronous target workflow state queries from the source process.
  - Given no target worker is polling; when cross-project create runs with Temporal reachable; then creation completes or reports start failure without a target `getState` query timeout.
- `rq-crossProjectDiskOrphanReconcile01` — Active disk-only changes can be reseeded into workflows; archived/closed records are not resurrected.
  - Given a non-terminal disk projection with no workflow; when bounded reconciliation or read-time reseed runs; then a workflow starts from disk. Given archived/closed disk state; then no workflow is recreated.
- `rq-crossProjectTemporalFailure01` — Temporal-start failure rolls back partial target scaffolding and fails clearly.
  - Given target disk scaffolding begins but Temporal start fails; when create returns; then the target active change does not remain as disk-only state and the response never claims live registration.

### Related Pattern Scan

- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` cross-project create is the only found target-change creation path using `createLegacyStore` for mutation.
- `plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts` target mutations already centralize trust gating and Temporal-backed target store creation.
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts` already has non-terminal disk reseed behavior, so a separate broad reconciler may be unnecessary.

### LBP Check

- Internal architecture change; no external tool/library alternative is viable.
- Temporal docs/source support `workflow.start` with `workflowId`/`taskQueue`, one active workflow per ID, and explicit conflict handling. Existing ADV `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` already wraps this behavior.
- Long-term best practice: reuse target-store and workflow-start helpers so correctness stays structural and tested.

### Discovery Opportunity Scout

- Scout ran via `adv-researcher`; report submitted.
- Auto-adopt: reuse `startChangeWorkflow`/`changeSeedStateFromChange` rather than bespoke seed assembly.
- User/design surfaced choices resolved: rollback/fail on Temporal failure; reconcile all active disk-only records; investigate exact trigger in design.

### User Decisions

- Temporal-start failure after scaffold begins: rollback and fail.
- Orphan reconciliation breadth: all active records; not a one-time `reworkRelatedCards` repair.
- Recovery trigger: needs design investigation; choose the trigger by source evidence and performance/safety tradeoff.

### AMBIGUITY ANALYSIS

Coverage: B:C F:C S:C M:C

No blocking ambiguity findings.

### Recommended Objectives

1. Cross-project create live-registers target changes in Temporal Visibility without requiring a target worker query.
2. Existing active disk-only target changes are recoverable through bounded, non-terminal-only reseeding.
3. `target_path` trust and canonical target shard routing remain intact.
4. Temporal failure behavior is explicit: rollback/fail, no active disk-only orphan.
5. Specs/tests pin the contract so future cross-project create paths cannot regress to disk-only.
documents.problemStatement:
Cross-project change creation (`adv_change_create` with `target_path`) writes the target project's change only to disk and never starts its Temporal change workflow. In `plugin/src/tools/change.ts` the cross-repo path uses `createLegacyStore(target_path, …)` with an explicit comment that "Temporal runtime is not initialized for external repos in this tool path." It calls `targetStore.changes.create(...)` and persists cross-project metadata, but does not call `startChangeWorkflow` or `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` for the target project.

Since the CLI status read is Temporal-Visibility-backed (`rq-statusCliWorkerFree01`), a change with no Temporal workflow has no Visibility search attributes and therefore does not appear in `adv status --json` or launcher ADV rows. Confirmed context: `reworkRelatedCards` exists as a disk-only change in pokeedge but has no `adv/change/<projectId>/reworkRelatedCards` workflow and no `AdvChangeId='reworkRelatedCards'` Visibility entry.

Starting a workflow in the target project fits the current architecture: ADV projects share one Temporal namespace, isolation is by workflow id and task queue, `client.workflow.start` does not require a live target worker, and `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` is idempotent and can hydrate from disk. The unsafe path is querying the target workflow synchronously from the source process; this change must avoid that.

Desired outcome: cross-project create registers the target change workflow so live status and launcher surfaces can see it, and existing active disk-only target changes can be reconciled into Temporal-backed visibility.

# Design

## Architecture Overview

Cross-project create currently diverges from the target-path mutation architecture: `createCrossProjectFollowUp()` opens a target `createLegacyStore`, writes disk state, patches cross-project origin metadata, and returns. It never starts a target change workflow.

The design converges cross-project create on existing Temporal-backed target-store machinery:

1. Resolve the target through `withTargetPathStore(... stateRequirement: "temporal-required")` so target trust gating, canonical target shard routing, target project id, and target Temporal client are one source of truth.
2. Pass all creation metadata before `Store.changes.create()` starts the workflow, so the target workflow seed contains provenance and the disk projection is not patched after start.
3. Reuse `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` from the Temporal store; do not add direct workflow-start code in `change.ts`.
4. Make existing read/list reseed behavior the deliberate reconciliation trigger for active disk-only records.
5. Keep CLI status read-only and Visibility-backed. Reconciliation is a Temporal-backed store/session concern, not a disk fallback in `adv status`.

## Key Decisions

### KD1 — Route cross-project create through Temporal-backed target store

Use `withTargetPathStore({ target_path, stateRequirement: "temporal-required" })` for `adv_change_create target_path`.

Rationale:

- `target-project.ts` already validates target paths, resolves target project id, preserves canonical target shard routing, and initializes a Temporal-backed target store.
- Temporal-backed `changes.create()` already calls `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` with `projectionChangesDir`, `archiveProjects`, workflow id, task queue, and seed state.
- This avoids bespoke Temporal client construction inside `change.ts` and preserves structural correctness.

Design detail:

- Extend `adv_change_create` target-path arguments with optional `target_confirmed` and `confirmationEvidence` if target-store routing requires them.
- Trusted related repos proceed without extra confirmation; untrusted target mutation preserves the existing target-project confirmation gate.

### KD2 — Seed cross-project origin before workflow start

Current code patches `change.cross_project_origin` after create via `changes.save()`. That is not sufficient for a workflow-owned target change because non-archived `save()` is a disk/overlay update, not an authoritative workflow-state mutation.

Design:

- Extend `ChangeCreateInitialMetadata` to include `cross_project_origin`.
- Extend `ChangeWorkflowState` seed support and workflow-state-to-change mapping to carry `cross_project_origin` as a replay-safe optional field.
- Wire `cross_project_origin` into both seed paths:
  - the inline `seedState` object in `store-temporal/changes.ts` create path;
  - `changeSeedStateFromChange()` for reseed/import paths.
- Build the origin section into proposal content as before, but pass `cross_project_origin` as initial metadata before `changes.create()` starts the workflow.
- Remove the post-create target `changes.get()`/`changes.save()` patch from cross-project create.

### KD3 — No target workflow query from source process

Cross-project create must not call target `changes.get()` after create, because a workflow query can require a target worker. The source process should:

- call target `changes.create()`;
- rely on workflow start and content signals only;
- return the known `changeId`, target path, and origin metadata from local inputs/results;
- update source-side `cross_project_links` in the current store only.

### KD4 — Temporal-start failure rolls back/fails

Use the existing Temporal store create semantics: if `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` fails after disk scaffolding, remove the target change directory and rethrow/report the original failure. This matches the approved agreement and same-project create behavior.

Implementation adds explicit tests that the target active change is absent after simulated workflow-start failure and the response does not claim live registration.

### KD5 — Existing read/list reseed is the reconciliation trigger

No new broad scanner is needed. `listResolvedChanges()` already unions disk ids with Visibility ids, then loads each id through `getTemporalChange()`. `getTemporalChange()` classifies missing workflow reads and calls `reseedChangeFromDisk()` for non-terminal disk projections. `reseedChangeFromDisk()` skips `archived` and `closed` records.

Design action:

- Treat this as the intended reconciliation trigger.
- Add tests proving `changes.list()` / target Temporal store list on disk-only active records starts workflows and returns live state.
- Add tests proving archived/closed disk records are not recreated.
- Keep the existing batch size (`CHANGE_LIST_BATCH_SIZE = 20`) as the automatic bound; add targeted checks that one missing workflow does not abort the batch.

This satisfies the user decision: all active records can be recovered by normal target ADV list/read flows, not a one-off repair. It also avoids adding mutation authority to the CLI status path.

### KD6 — Optional future explicit repair reuses same helper

If future UX needs a manual repair tool, it should call the same store-owned reseed/list helper path and not duplicate scanning logic. This change only makes the existing behavior deliberate and tested.

### KD7 — Keep worker-free status semantics unchanged

`adv status --json` remains live Temporal Visibility-backed. It must not scan disk active records as a substitute source. Once cross-project create starts workflows and list/read reconciliation reseeds old active records, the existing Visibility status path sees them normally.

## ADR Drafts

None. Decisions converge on existing target-store and Temporal-store architecture and are reversible through normal helper-level refactor.

## Implementation Strategy

1. **Schema/metadata path**
   - Add optional `target_confirmed` and `confirmationEvidence` fields to `adv_change_create` if target-store routing requires them.
   - Add `cross_project_origin` to `ChangeCreateInitialMetadata`.
   - Add `cross_project_origin` to `ChangeWorkflowState` seed picklist, inline create `seedState`, `changeSeedStateFromChange()`, and workflow-state-to-change mapping.
2. **Cross-project create path**
   - Replace direct `createLegacyStore` in `createCrossProjectFollowUp()` with `withTargetPathStore(... stateRequirement: "temporal-required")`.
   - Pass enriched proposal artifacts plus `initialMetadata: { cross_project_origin }`.
   - Remove target post-create `get()`/`save()` patch.
   - Keep source-side outbound link update.
3. **Reconciliation tests / hardening**
   - Add tests around existing `listResolvedChanges()` → `getTemporalChange()` → `reseedChangeFromDisk()` path.
   - Confirm non-terminal disk-only changes reseed into workflows.
   - Confirm archived/closed changes short-circuit and do not start workflows.
   - Confirm batch/list behavior remains bounded and orphan-tolerant.
4. **Tests**
   - Cross-project create starts target workflow through target Temporal store.
   - Workflow start receives target project id, target queue/search attrs, and cross-project origin seed.
   - No target `getState` query occurs in source process.
   - Workflow-start failure rolls back target active disk projection and reports failure.
   - Existing non-terminal disk-only records reseed; archived/closed records do not.
   - Target trust gating and canonical shard tests from `fixTargetPathSharding` remain green.
5. **Spec law**
   - Add requirements for cross-project create workflow registration, no target query, active-only reconciliation, and rollback/failure behavior.

## LBP Analysis

This design uses existing structural sources of truth:

- `withTargetPathStore()` for target trust, target project id, target external root, and target Temporal service.
- Temporal-backed `changes.create()` for workflow start and rollback.
- `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted()` for idempotent start/search-attribute registration.
- `listResolvedChanges()`/`getTemporalChange()`/`reseedChangeFromDisk()` for missing workflow recovery.
- Visibility status remains the read model; disk is not reintroduced as active status source.

Rejected alternatives:

- Direct `client.workflow.start` in `change.ts`: duplicates target-store setup, trust gating, target shard routing, and seed construction.
- CLI-status disk scan + reseed: would add mutation authority to a read-only worker-free CLI path and weaken fail-closed semantics.
- New startup scanner: existing list/read reseed already provides bounded recovery; extra scanner adds latency and duplicate logic.
- One-off `reworkRelatedCards` repair: fixes symptom only and violates the approved future-proof scope.

## Affected Components

- `plugin/src/tools/change.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/target-project.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-types.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/index.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/contracts.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/change-state.ts`
- `plugin/src/temporal/observability.ts` tests around start attrs
- `plugin/src/tools/cross-project-coordination.test.ts`
- `plugin/src/tools/target-project.test.ts`
- `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/*.test.ts`
- `.adv/specs/advance-workflow/spec.json` or `.adv/specs/advance-meta/spec.json`

## Design-Derived Criteria

1. Cross-project create must not call target `changes.get()` or any target workflow query after create.
2. Cross-project origin metadata must be present in workflow state after creation, not only in disk projection or overlay cache.
3. Reconciliation trigger is `changes.list()`/normal Temporal-backed target store reads through existing reseed path; CLI status remains read-only.
4. Reconciliation must skip `archived` and `closed` records before workflow start.
5. List/reseed behavior remains bounded by existing batch loading and must not abort the whole list when one orphan fails.
6. `stateRequirement` values must use the actual enum value `"temporal-required"`.

## Risks / Mitigations

- **Risk:** `adv_change_create` target-path schema changes can break existing callers.
  - Mitigation: fields are optional; only untrusted target mutations require confirmation per existing target policy.
- **Risk:** Cross-project origin disappears from workflow-owned reads.
  - Mitigation: seed `cross_project_origin` before workflow start and map it through workflow state, including the inline create seed block.
- **Risk:** Existing CLI launcher cannot heal old orphans without a target ADV list/read path first.
  - Mitigation: preserve CLI read-only contract; new cross-project creates no longer need healing, and normal target ADV list/read/session paths self-heal old active records.
- **Risk:** Already-started workflows produce duplicate errors.
  - Mitigation: reuse `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` idempotency; tests cover duplicate/reconcile reruns.
- **Risk:** Trust gate asymmetry remains if `adv_change_create` does not expose confirmation fields.
  - Mitigation: add optional confirmation fields or explicitly prove target is trusted in tests before using temporal target store.

## Design Leverage Scout

Scout result: VALIDATED with adopted leverage points.

Auto-adopted:

- Use existing list/read-time reseed as reconciliation trigger; no new scan surface.
- Reuse `ensureChangeWorkflowStarted` / seed helpers, not bespoke workflow-start code.
- Keep rollback/fail semantics aligned with existing P1.4 Temporal create behavior.

Surfaced/handled in design:

- Central architecture decision is routing cross-project create through `withTargetPathStore` while preserving source-side `cross_project_links` and target origin metadata.
- Trust-gate unification remains implementation-sensitive; preserve existing policy and add optional create confirmation fields if needed.

## Validator Result

Validator: VALIDATED with two non-blocking cautions; both incorporated.

- Caution 1: `cross_project_origin` must be wired into the inline `seedState` block in `store-temporal/changes.ts`, not only `changeSeedStateFromChange()`.
- Caution 2: use actual `TargetStateRequirement` enum value `"temporal-required"`, not shorthand `"temporal"`.

# Executive Summary

## Outcome
Cross-project change creation now uses target Temporal workflow state instead of disk-only target scaffolds. Active disk-only target records are recoverable through the normal list/read reseed path, while archived/closed records remain terminal projections.

## Verdict
APPROVED

## What Was Built
1. Threaded `cross_project_origin`, `cross_project_links`, and `external_dependencies` through Temporal workflow state, seed, continue-as-new, read mapping, and coordination update signals.
2. Routed `adv_change_create target_path` through `withTargetPathStore({ stateRequirement: "temporal-required" })` with target confirmation evidence and target-context project IDs.
3. Removed post-create target get/save patching; target create seeds provenance before workflow start and failure returns an error without writing source links.
4. Added bounded tests for target routing, no target getState query, failure semantics, active disk-only reseed, terminal skip behavior, preflight policy, and spec law.
5. Updated `advance-workflow` spec law for cross-project Temporal create and active-only reconciliation.

## What Was Verified
- Verdict: READY from `adv-reviewer`; review remediation applied for cross-project link/dependency metadata preservation.
- Tests: `pnpm run check` passed; targeted Vitest suite passed (184 tests) after review remediation.
- Preview URL: not_applicable — backend/tooling workflow change; no browser-visible or visual output surface.
- Contract matrix: 23 required rows passed/respected; 0 failed/violated/unknown.

## Remaining Concerns
Live ADV tool behavior requires build/deploy/restart before in-session MCP calls use the new source, because OpenCode loads deployed `dist` at session startup. No product-scope concerns remain.
